_ai_insights.EXEC_SUMMARY_GOOD: Network Rail has built a large and growing Copilot user base — 17,458 users across 50 organisations, with monthly active users rising 68% from July to November 2025. Licensed users show strong retention at 96% month-over-month, and the engagement premium is striking: licensed users generate 10x more prompts than unlicensed chat users.
_ai_insights.EXEC_SUMMARY_GAP: Only 18% of Copilot users hold a license, meaning the vast majority interact through unlicensed chat only. Unlicensed retention sits at 59% — nearly 3,000 users churn each month. The agent ecosystem, while showing early promise with 966 users, remains concentrated around two 1P agents (Researcher and Analyst account for 68% of agent users).
_ai_insights.EXEC_SUMMARY_OPP: The 14,262 unlicensed chat users represent a significant licensing opportunity. With unlicensed users averaging 69 prompts each, the most active among them are already demonstrating need. Targeting the top quartile of unlicensed users for license conversion could immediately improve both coverage and habitual usage rates.
_ai_insights.INSIGHT_REACH: Network Rail has achieved full activation of its 3,196 licensed seats — every provisioned license is in active use. However, license coverage tells a different story: only 18.3% of the 17,458 active users hold a license. The remaining 14,262 users have found their way to Copilot through unlicensed chat, creating a large but lightly engaged user base. Agent adoption stands at 5.5%, with 966 users across 1,169 distinct agents. The 50 organisations show wide variation — Finance & Business Support leads agent adoption at 25.5%, while larger groups like Regions (648 users) sit at 18.5%.
_ai_insights.INSIGHT_HABIT: Licensed users have developed strong habits — 26.7% are habitual users (11+ active days per month), and in October 2025, a remarkable 45% of licensed users hit 11-19 active days. This dropped to 26% in December, likely reflecting seasonal patterns. Licensed retention is excellent at 96.2%. The contrast with unlicensed users is sharp: only 4.8% are habitual, and retention is 58.9%. Agent habit formation is early-stage — just 1.8% of agent users reach 11+ days.
_ai_insights.INSIGHT_SKILL: Users are going deep across multiple surfaces — the average user touches 2.3 distinct app surfaces, with BizChat (66%), Outlook (11%), Edge (8%), and Word (8%) leading. An impressive 97.8% of sessions involve multiple turns, suggesting users are having substantive conversations rather than one-off queries. Agent users engage with an average of 2.6 different agents, indicating exploration across the portfolio. The agent return rate of 93.7% shows that once users try agents, they come back.
_ai_insights.SUBTITLE_REACH: Every license is active, but only 18% of users have one
_ai_insights.SUBTITLE_HABIT: 96% licensed retention, but unlicensed churn is high
_ai_insights.SUBTITLE_VALUE: Licensed users generate 691 prompts vs 69 for unlicensed
_ai_insights.SPOTLIGHT_HABIT: The gap between licensed and unlicensed engagement is the defining feature of Network Rail's adoption. Licensed users are deeply embedded — 26.7% use Copilot 11 or more days per month, and 96% return month-over-month. Unlicensed users, despite numbering 4.5x more, are far less sticky: 82% use it fewer than 6 days per month, and 41% churn each month. This creates a two-speed organisation where a small licensed core drives disproportionate value.
_ai_insights.SPOTLIGHT_MATURITY: Network Rail has built broad awareness of Copilot across the organisation — 17,458 users in 6 months is significant for a workforce of ~3,190. The challenge is not awareness but depth: converting the large unlicensed base into licensed, habitual users who generate the 10x engagement premium that licensed users already deliver.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_0: 17,458 users across 50 organisations — Copilot awareness is not the issue. License depth is.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_1: 100% license activation with only 18% coverage — every seat is used, but most users don't have one.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_2: Licensed users return 96% of the time and use Copilot 11+ days a month. Unlicensed users churn at 41%.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_3: 98% of sessions are multi-turn — users are having real conversations, not testing prompts.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_4: At 691 prompts per licensed user vs 69 for unlicensed, the licence pays for itself 10 times over.
_ai_insights.PULLQUOTE_5: Researcher and Analyst handle 93% of all agent sessions — the agent portfolio needs broadening.
_ai_insights.REC_1_TITLE: Convert Top Unlicensed Users to Licensed
_ai_insights.REC_1_DESC: The top quartile of the 14,262 unlicensed chat users are already demonstrating regular engagement. Identify users with 6+ active days and prioritise them for license provisioning. Based on the 10x engagement premium, each conversion should yield significant productivity gains.
_ai_insights.REC_2_DESC: With 41% monthly churn among unlicensed users (~2,900 users per month), a lightweight onboarding prompt — such as guided first prompts or surface-specific tips — could improve retention from 59% toward the licensed benchmark of 96%. Even a 10-point improvement retains 700+ additional users monthly.
_ai_insights.REC_3_TITLE: Broaden the Agent Portfolio Beyond Researcher
_ai_insights.REC_3_DESC: Researcher and Analyst account for 68% of agent users and 93% of sessions. Custom agents from Copilot Studio (like AM Strategy Assurance at 5,335 sessions from 25 users) show strong engagement but limited reach. Feature 3-5 high-performing custom agents in internal communications to drive discovery across the 50 organisations.
_ai_insights.REC_4_TITLE: Target Regions for Agent Adoption Push
_ai_insights.REC_4_DESC: Regions is the largest org with 648 users but only 18.5% agent adoption. Finance & Business Support achieves 25.5% with fewer users. Run an agent awareness campaign in Regions — with 648 potential users, even matching the 25% benchmark would add ~40 agent users and establish agent use in the largest part of the organisation.
